name: inngest-handler
description: Create and manage Inngest functions for reliable background jobs, workflows, and scheduled tasks.
capabilities: Create multi-step Inngest functions with step.run() for retriable operations
Implement durable pauses with step.sleep() and step.waitForEvent() for human interaction
Configure rate limiting, throttling, and debounce for API protection
Handle errors with automatic retries and NonRetriableError for fatal failures
Build parallel step execution for faster workflow completion
Implement failure handlers with rollback logic and alerting
author: AayushBaniya2006
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill containing markdown guides and TypeScript code examples for building Inngest workflows. The static scanner flagged backtick characters in markdown as shell command execution, but these are template literals in code blocks. No executable code, network calls, filesystem access, or command execution capabilities exist. All static findings are false positives.
valueStatement: Building reliable background jobs and scheduled tasks is complex. This skill provides patterns and best practices for Inngest workflows with durable execution, automatic retries, and event-driven architecture.
userTitle: Create Inngest workflows for background jobs
limitations: This skill provides patterns and documentation only; it does not execute Inngest functions directly
Requires an existing Inngest project setup with proper credentials configured
Does not generate complete project boilerplate or configure deployment pipelines

useCases.title: Process orders and payments
useCases.description: Build order fulfillment workflows that wait for payment confirmation, validate inventory, and send notifications automatically.

useCases.title: Automate DevOps pipelines
useCases.description: Create scheduled maintenance workflows with retry logic, health checks, and incident alerting for reliable operations.

useCases.title: Handle async data processing
useCases.description: Implement background data sync jobs that process files in parallel, with checkpointing for resumability.

promptTemplates.title: Basic workflow
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an Inngest function that processes user signups by sending a welcome email and creating a default workspace.

promptTemplates.title: Multi-step process
promptTemplates.prompt: Build an order processing workflow with validation, payment wait, inventory check, and shipping notification steps.

promptTemplates.title: Error handling
promptTemplates.prompt: Add failure handlers to my webhook processing function with automatic rollback and admin alerts on final failure.

promptTemplates.title: Rate limiting
promptTemplates.prompt: Configure rate limiting and throttling on my CRM sync function to prevent overwhelming third-party APIs.

bestPractices: Always wrap database writes and API calls in step.run() to ensure retriability and memoization
Use unique, stable step IDs - do not dynamically generate IDs unless inside loops with index
Return data from steps instead of mutating external variables for proper state management
antiPatterns: Do not use setTimeout() or setInterval() - use step.sleep() for durable pauses that persist across restarts
Do not place side effects outside step.run() - code runs on every retry and can cause duplicate operations
Do not forget to handle non-retriable errors - throwing NonRetriableError prevents wasted retry attempts

faq.question: Which Inngest versions are supported?
faq.answer: Supports current Inngest SDK with createFunction API. Check your package.json for @inngest/client or inngest package versions.

faq.question: What are the retry limits?
faq.answer: Default is 4-5 retries with exponential backoff. Configure with retries: N option in function config (0 to disable).

faq.question: How do I integrate with my existing codebase?
faq.answer: Import and export all functions from src/lib/inngest/functions/index.ts as shown in the registration section.

faq.question: Is my data safe with Inngest?
faq.answer: Inngest only stores event metadata and step output data. Sensitive data stays in your infrastructure with proper encryption.

faq.question: Why are my steps running multiple times?
faq.answer: Steps outside step.run() execute on every retry. Move all logic inside step.run() for memoization.

faq.question: How does this compare to other job queues?
faq.answer: Inngest provides durable execution with automatic retries, event-driven triggers, and built-in observability without separate workers.
